title: How I’ve been using AI to rethink the way we backtest and build trading strategies
author: Flat-Dragonfruit8746
selftext:
For the past few months, I’ve been working on a [tool](https://aiquantstudio.com/) that lets traders skip the usual coding steps and test ideas directly through plain English. You type something like “buy when RSI drops below 30 and price breaks a 10-day high,” and it runs a proper backtest using verified financial logic.

The goal was to eliminate the barrier between idea and validation -  something I noticed a lot of non-coders and even experienced traders struggled with. The system uses a combination of natural language processing, modular logic blocks, and a custom backtesting engine built for clarity and speed.

So far it’s helped early users save time, refine their setups, and avoid trading on gut instinct. No templates or drag-and-drop builders - just clean logic translation and actual results.

Curious how others here are blending AI into their workflow. Are you focused more on signal generation, automation, or evaluation? Where do you feel AI still needs to catch up in the trading space?
